title: Hotspots Summary
description: Top-5 function summary produced by `hotspots analyze --mode snapshot --explain-patterns`. Available at each post's report_json URL on reports.hotspots.dev.

sha.description: Full 40-character git commit SHA that was analyzed

analyzed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the analysis ran

hotspots_version.description: Version of the hotspots CLI that produced this snapshot

clone_depth.description: Git clone depth used — affects how far back commit history is considered when computing activity scores

top_risk.description: Activity-weighted risk score of the highest-ranked function in this snapshot

top.description: Top functions ranked by activity-weighted risk, descending. Typically 5 entries.

function.description: Function name. '<anonymous>' for arrow functions or unnamed expressions.

file.description: File path relative to the repo root

risk.description: Activity-weighted risk score for this function (complexity × recent commit activity). Different from lrs — see quadrant and touches_30d for true activity.

lrs.description: Local Risk Score — structural complexity only (CC, ND, FO, NS combined). Never reaches zero even for untouched functions because the decay is applied to risk, not to lrs directly.

quadrant.description: Activity × complexity quadrant. 'fire' = high complexity AND high recent activity (live regression risk). 'debt' = high complexity but low recent activity (structural debt). Always check this field — a high risk score alone does not mean a function is actively changing.

touches_30d.description: Number of commits that touched this function in the last 30 days. Use together with quadrant to determine true activity level.

days_since_changed.description: Days elapsed since this function was last modified in git history.

patterns.description: Antipattern labels detected on this function. Empty array if none.

pattern_counts.description: Aggregate count of each antipattern label across all top functions

top_patterns.description: Antipattern labels sorted by frequency (most common first)
